$comment: Network + CCTP parameters. Values marked verified were confirmed against
Circle's docs on 2026-07-19. Values marked UNCONFIRMED do not exist yet
because Arc mainnet has not launched — the bot discovers them at runtime
and refuses to act on guesses. Do not hardcode a mainnet chainId here.

destination.name: Arc

knownWrongChainIds.$comment: Guard rails. Detection MUST reject these.
knownWrongChainIds.1243: Unrelated project also called ARC (native currency ARC, not USDC)

$cctpComment: Leave cctpDomain null: the bot reads it from the destination chain itself
by calling localDomain() on the MessageTransmitter, which is authoritative.
Set it only if you want to PIN an expected value — a mismatch then aborts.
The contract addresses below are CCTP v2's canonical deploy addresses.
Verified 2026-07-19 against live RPCs: identical on Base, Arbitrum and
Optimism, with localDomain() returning 6, 3 and 2 as documented. Arc is
expected to deploy at the same addresses. The bot verifies bytecode and
localDomain() before trusting them, so a wrong guess fails safe.
$chainIdComment: 5042 is Arc mainnet, registered in the ethereum-lists/chains registry as
'Arc' / shortName 'arc-mainnet' with infoURL https://arc.network - the same
project as the 5042002 testnet entry. Verified 2026-07-19.
The registry entry has an EMPTY rpc[] and explorers[] list: the id is
reserved ahead of launch, the network is not switched on yet.
Pinning it here makes detection strict - the bot will now accept ONLY
chain 5042 as Arc mainnet and reject anything else, instead of trusting
whatever a candidate RPC claims to be.

$comment: Arc uses USDC as its native gas token, but the NATIVE unit is 18 decimals
like any EVM chain - not 6. Confirmed against the ethereum-lists registry
for both 5042 (mainnet) and 5042002 (testnet, which is live).
The ERC20 USDC contract is still 6 decimals. Do not mix them up: native
gas balances use 18, token balances use 6.
nativeCurrency.name: USD Coin

$comment2: Forwarding (depositForBurnWithHook) lets Circle relay and pay destination gas.
This matters enormously on Arc: USDC IS the gas token, so a fresh wallet has
zero gas until the mint lands. The direct path would strand funds.
